titel: Compensation rules with the victim of crime at the center.
html:
When a crime cannot be prevented, societal resources should focus on mitigating the consequences of the crime and assisting the victim. Those who are victims of crime can suffer various damages, and financial compensation for these damages is an important part of the victim's recovery. The legitimacy of the compensation system requires that victims not only have the right to compensation for their damages but also have good opportunities to actually receive that compensation. Therefore, the government proposes several legislative changes to strengthen the position of victims and reduce the consequences of crime. These proposals include:

  • Simplifying and shortening the process for victims to receive compensation for damages caused by crime.
  • Victims should be able to receive such compensation from the National Authority for Victims of Crime based on a court judgment for damages without first having to contact the perpetrator.
  • Increasing the liability of guardians for damages caused by their children through criminal acts.
  • Facilitating the voluntary payment of compensation for damages caused by crime.
  • Limiting the prohibitions on the seizure of certain payments to a greater extent, in order to make it more possible to seize assets to benefit victims.

The government also proposes legislative changes to achieve a more reasonable and efficient system for compensation in cases of detention.

These legislative changes are proposed to come into effect on September 1, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Government Proposal:

  1. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Code of Judicial Procedure.
  2. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Family Code.
  3. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Code of Enforcement.
  4. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Tort Law (1972:207).
  5. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Traffic Accident Law (1975:1410).
  6. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Act (1998:714) on compensation for deprivation of liberty and other coercive measures.
  7. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Insurance Contract Act (2005:104).
  8. The Parliament approves the government's proposed amendment to the Criminal Injury Compensation Act (2014:322).
tldr: The government wants to amend the laws to make it easier and faster for individuals who have been victims of crime to receive financial compensation for their damages. The proposals aim to help victims of crime receive the right support and financial assistance more quickly.
lattlast:
The government has proposed new laws to help individuals who have been victims of crime. The goal is to reduce the serious consequences of crime and ensure that those who have been harmed receive the compensation they are entitled to. This will be achieved, in part, by amending several different laws in Sweden.

A key element is to expedite the process of receiving funds from the Swedish National Authority for Victims of Crime, which is the agency responsible for handling compensation. The proposal suggests that individuals should be able to receive funds directly if they have a court judgment stating that they are entitled to compensation, without first having to attempt to recover the funds directly from the person who committed the crime.

The proposals also include increased responsibility for parents to pay compensation if their children commit crimes. It will also become easier for individuals to voluntarily repay compensation. Furthermore, the government wants to amend the rules regarding garnishment, which means that authorities can more easily seize assets from individuals to provide compensation to victims of crime.

If the Parliament votes in favor of the proposals, the new rules will come into effect on September 1, 2026. The proposals cover a wide range of laws, including laws related to compensation, traffic accidents, and how parental responsibility is handled.
